issue: 'truly-novel base family' was defined against the FUNGAL 2024 subset only; a family absent from fungal 2024 can still exist in all-kingdom CAZy 2024 (bacteria/plants/etc.). Such proteins are NEW-TO-FUNGI, not new CAZyme families.
test: Re-scored the 726 novel_family sequences against the FULL all-kingdom 2024 CAZy (DIAMOND).
revised_interpretation: Only CBM104 is absent from all-kingdom 2024 CAZy. The other 5 of the original 6 'truly-novel base families' (CBM3, CBM8, GT109, GT119, PL29) existed in 2024 CAZy in non-fungal kingdoms. The correct 'genuinely novel to CAZy 2024' set is ~6 sequences, dominated by CBM104.
